page: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server configured with an x86_64 architecture. It features 24 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8570 processor running at 2.0 GHz, with 24 physical cores and a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 240 GB of system memory, providing 10 GB of RAM per core. It integrates a single NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB of dedicated VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across multiple vectors. The server achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression at 34.65 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.55 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, reaching 4156.98 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 251.17 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For artificial intelligence workloads, the platform demonstrates top-tier LLM inference speeds, processing 9953.26 tokens per second for prompts and generating 525.92 tokens per second using a small 135M model.

From a resource density and cost perspective, this server represents a high-end hardware profile designed for specialized acceleration. The combination of 240 GB of system memory and 192 GB of GPU VRAM offers a balanced ratio for large-scale models, though the shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage require external storage solutions. This configuration is highly suited for deep learning, LLM inference, data science, and advanced cryptographic or mathematical simulations.
description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 24 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8570 processor and 240 GB of system memory. The hardware is anchored by a single NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB of VRAM, operating on an x86_64 architecture under KVM virtualization. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and LLM inference for small models, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operations. The instance does not include local storage, necessitating network-attached storage configurations. This server is engineered for demanding workloads such as machine learning inference, data science, and parallel computing tasks that leverage massive GPU memory.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 240 GB of RAM, and an NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB of VRAM for high-performance computing.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 features 24 Intel vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and a 192 GB NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU for accelerated computing.
tagline: A high-capacity accelerated server featuring a next-generation graphics processor and extensive memory for intensive parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB of dedicated VRAM.
24 Intel Xeon vCPUs with 240 GB of system memory.
Top-tier LLM inference and single-threaded compression performance.
No local storage included, requiring external storage solutions.
Best suited for machine learning, data science, and parallel processing.
